global_layoffs.description: CEO Jack Dorsey cut nearly half the workforce citing AI efficiency gains, predicting most companies will follow within a year.
Corporate restructuring to flatten management and redirect resources toward AI and agentic systems. Part of 30K cuts since October 2025.
Cuts across marketing, product, data analytics, and Agentforce AI teams. Previously cut ~4,000 customer support roles after deploying AI systems.
10% of Reality Labs division cut as Meta pivots from Metaverse to phones and AI after $60B in losses.
15% of team laid off, attributed in part to a strategic shift toward AI-powered discovery and ad products.
Accelerating plan to replace 10,000 jobs with AI by end of decade, primarily in customer service and network operations.
Continuing restructuring of 8,000 roles as part of Joule AI transformation strategy, converting positions to AI engineering.
AI chatbots fully replaced 700 customer service agents. CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowski cited AI as primary driver and plans further reductions.
Continued AI-first pivot eliminating roles across tax preparation and customer support as AI handles majority of user queries.
Further cuts as ChatGPT continues disrupting core homework-help business. Stock down 95% from peak.
AI fully replaces remaining human translators and content creators, completing AI-first content pipeline.

gulf_mena_automation.description: Saudi Arabia ranked #1 globally in Public Sector AI Adoption Index 2026, scoring 66/100 across 324 surveyed public servants.
Signed MOU with Qualcomm to deliver advanced AI data centers, hybrid AI across edge devices and cloud, and AI services to KSA.
AI supercomputing hub in UAE (with OpenAI, G42, Microsoft) plans 1 GW data center cluster, 200 MW operational by 2026.
Expanding AI-driven predictive maintenance and autonomous drilling systems across upstream operations.
AI customer service platform now handles 80%+ of routine inquiries, reducing frontline staffing needs.
Expanded AI deployment for fraud detection, loan processing, and compliance automation across all branches.
Secured $500M financing to expand data center and AI operations, constructing 120MW capacity over 5 years.
Robotic warehouse fulfillment and AI demand forecasting scaling across MENA operations.
AI-powered dispatch, dynamic routing, and automated customer support reducing operational headcount.
Planning AI-native city operations with autonomous transport, robotic construction, and agentic building management.

saudi_policy_updates.title: SAMAI 2 Workforce AI Initiative Expanded
saudi_policy_updates.description: SDAIA confirmed 1.1M Saudi citizens trained through SAMAI programme. SAMAI 2 focuses on sector-specific AI applications across ministries.

saudi_policy_updates.title: Saudi Arabia #1 in Global Public Sector AI Adoption
saudi_policy_updates.description: KSA scored 66/100 in Public Sector AI Adoption Index 2026, placing first globally. However, 50% of public servants report training is compliance-focused, not capability-building.

saudi_policy_updates.title: PIF $40B AI Investment Fund Active
saudi_policy_updates.description: Saudi sovereign wealth fund's $40B AI investment fund now actively deploying capital into semiconductors, data centers, and AI companies via HUMAIN.

saudi_policy_updates.title: Engineering sector Saudization raised to 30%
saudi_policy_updates.description: HRSD raised engineering profession Saudization quota to 30% in phase 2, increasing pressure on expat engineering roles.

saudi_policy_updates.title: HRDF AI Reskilling Track Active
saudi_policy_updates.description: HRDF's dedicated AI reskilling programs for workers in at-risk sectors now enrolling across retail, finance, and admin sectors.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Block CEO predicts most companies will halve workforce for AI within a year
ai_workforce_signals.description: Jack Dorsey's letter to shareholders after cutting 4,000 jobs stated that within 12 months, the majority of companies will make similar structural changes. Block shares soared 24% on the announcement.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: 55% of US hiring managers expect AI-driven layoffs in 2026
ai_workforce_signals.description: Resume.org survey of 1,000 US hiring managers found 55% expect layoffs in 2026, with 44% citing AI as the top driver. Oxford Economics warns of widespread AI-washing in layoff justifications.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: Saudi Arabia leads world in government AI adoption
ai_workforce_signals.description: Public Sector AI Adoption Index 2026 surveyed 3,335 public servants across 10 countries. KSA scored highest at 66/100, but 52% say training comes too late and 51% lack time for AI learning.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: GCC AI adoption reaches 84%, agentic AI emerging as next frontier
ai_workforce_signals.description: McKinsey reports GCC AI adoption hit 84% in 2025. SDAIA now focusing on agentic AI — systems that plan and execute complex tasks autonomously — as the next phase beyond generative models.

ai_workforce_signals.headline: 64% of UAE companies plan to increase automation investment
ai_workforce_signals.description: PwC Middle East survey shows 64% of UAE companies will increase automation spending in 2026. Top priorities: finance, HR, and customer operations — all high-employment sectors for Gulf expats.

weekly_stats.notable_trend: Block's 4,000-person AI-driven cut marks a watershed moment — first S&P 500 company to explicitly halve workforce citing AI, with CEO predicting industry-wide adoption within 12 months.
